title: Coarse Parallel Processing Using a Work Queue — Create a container image
text:
Now you are ready to create an image that you will run as a Job.
The job will use the
amqp-consume
utility to read the message
from the queue and run the actual work. Here is a very simple
example program:
#!/usr/bin/env python
# Just prints standard out and sleeps for 10 seconds.
import
sys
import
time
print
(
"Processing "
+
sys
.
stdin
.
readlines
()[
0
])
time
.
sleep
(
10
)
Give the script execution permission:
chmod +x worker.py
Now, build an image. Make a temporary directory, change to it,
download the
Dockerfile
,
and
worker.py
. In either case,
build the image with this command:
docker build -t job-wq-1 .
For the
Docker Hub
, tag your app image with
your username and push to the Hub with the below commands. Replace
<username>
with your Hub username.
docker tag job-wq-1 <username>/job-wq-1
docker push <username>/job-wq-1
If you are using an alternative container image registry, tag the
image and push it there instead.
